text: Readily available $o$-vinylphenols undergo a formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition with alkynes in the presence of catalytic [Cp*RhCl$_2$]$_2$ and Cu(OAc)$_2$. This transformation involves the selective cleavage of the terminal C–H bond of the alkenyl moiety, providing a practical, versatile, and atom-economical route to highly valuable benzoxepine skeletons. Furthermore, substituting the alkyne with carbon monoxide as the reaction partner enables a formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition, yielding coumarin products. This methodology represents a rare example of using C–H functionalization to assemble seven-membered heterocycles from simple precursors.
